NORO|CA NOW A


SUFFRAG|ST

WILL DEVOTE REST OF HER
LIFE TO CAUSE AFTER FIN
ISHlNG PRESENT GRAND
OPERA TOUR, WHICH IS HER
LAST.

pages.raw_text:
Madame Nordica has joined the
suffragettes. 1t has been many
day since a woman's cause has
gained so important recruit and
the news will spread rejoicing
among the battling suffragettes the
world over.


The credit of her conversion to
the woman's suffrage cause is due
to Mrs. Clarence Mackay. They
we're fellow passengers aboard the
Kaiser William II, which arrived at
Cherbourg lately. Madame Nor
IDEAS one companion on shipboard
was Mrs. Mackay, with whom she
spent hours walking the deck, deep
In conversation or porting over suf-
fragist literature in the salon. The
day before landing Madame Nor
dica said she had been wholly con-
WINGED of the justice of the cause.


ut is the one cause worth living
for and working for she said.
And ] mean to give the rest of my
life to it. Next season will be the
last on the opera and concert stage.
shall say farewell to the public,
many times in the near future to
plead for the cause of woman


So anxious was the great singer
to- get in touch with the English
leaders of the suffragist movement
that she left Immediately for Lon
don. Incidentally, she will sing in
London as we'll as in Paris before
returning to the United States,
where she is to make a farewell
tour, which will be followed by her
marriage to George Young. the New
York banker. Then she expects to
take up at once the new career
which has opened before her.


Nordica, who is New England
woman, has strong opinions and the
courage of her convictions. She is
an eloquent speaker and her en-
thusiasm, her wide popularity and
forceful personality will make her a
most valuable addition to the fight
ing battalion of women refOrmers.
